name: Gary Peacock
abstract: Gary George Peacock (May 12, 1935 – September 4, 2020) was an American jazz double bassist. He recorded a dozen albums under his own name, and also performed and recorded with major jazz figures such as avant garde saxophonist Albert Ayler, pianists Bill Evans, Paul Bley and Marilyn Crispell, and as a part of Keith Jarrett’s “Standards T…

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: It was great to play with Gary and Benny... Keith and I were asked by Gary Peacock to come do his record for ECM... Manfred thought it might be a good idea if maybe we formed a trio... The Out-Of-Towners with Keith Jarrett and Gary Peacock, essentially The Standards Trio
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I remember having a conversation about three years ago with Gary Peacock. We were both doing the Montreal Festival. And I had never met him before and I had a really fascinating conversation with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 25, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: Gary Peacock was a friend and he really liked my playing... I could always play easily with Gary Peacock
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I had my old bands, other good musicians, Keith Jarrett and Gary Peacock’s Standards Trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: some of the wonderful Paul Bley, Gary Peacock, and that kind of music.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: Julian Priester had been there, Gary Peacock had taught there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025
